acceptance_criteria_review: Pass: the new test proves validate_asset_references now fails when a question-bank canonical image path exists on disk but is absent from asset_manifest.v1.json.
Pass: the emitted missing_question_bank_manifest_path issue includes question_id, field, and path.
Pass: the pre-existing happy-path asset manifest validation fixture still passes.
Pass: no output/, data/, or input/ generated artifacts were modified by this iteration.
test_quality_review: The test is meaningful: it uses real temporary files, builds an asset manifest through the production writer, mutates the manifest into a stale/incomplete state, and calls the public validate_asset_references function. It avoids mocks and failed before the implementation. Risk: because question-bank records can carry the same path in canonical, singular, and list fields, the validator may emit repeated errors for one physical missing manifest path.
repo_hygiene_review: The iteration stayed within source/test scope and wrote loop artifacts under .agent-runs/2026-06-18T22-15-18-167Z/iteration-01/. The repo began dirty with many unrelated modified files and untracked loop/alignment files. The .agent-loop new-run script also created .agent-runs/latest, run-state.json, and empty iteration directories outside iteration-01 as part of its standard setup. The live output area is incomplete for the asset-reference gate: output/json/question_bank.json exists, but output/json/asset_manifest.v1.json and output/asterion/exports/latest are absent.
scope_creep_review: No broad extraction, rendering, alignment, export, dependency, or data-format work was added. The implementation is limited to validate_asset_references and one regression test.
regression_risks: Consumers of validate_asset_references may now see additional failures when the manifest is stale relative to question_bank.json; this is intended but could expose existing output hygiene debt.
The same missing manifest path may be reported once per source field occurrence.
Because src/exam_bank/asset_manifest.py had pre-existing dirty changes, review should separate this iteration's manifest-path check from earlier asset-id changes.
